title: 6 takeaways from the U.S. World Cup win over Bosnia-Herzegovina
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The U.S. men's soccer team advanced to the World Cup Round of 16 with a 2-0 win over Bosnia-Herzegovina in San Francisco.
Folarin Balogun and Malik Tillman scored for the U.S., with Balogun later receiving a controversial red card in the second half.
This victory marks the U.S. team's first knockout stage win at a men's World Cup since 2002, ending a 10-game losing streak against European teams.

title: Brighton man charged in 2025 killing of 15-year-old in Allston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Armani Collazos, 26, of Brighton, charged in June 2025 shooting death of 15-year-old Phillip Franco in Allston.
Collazos faces multiple charges including murder and assault, and is held without bail after pleading not guilty.
Miranda Bodden, 22, charged as accessory, released on $2,000 bail; both set to return to court August 8.

title: Bus driver strike disrupts bus service in Merrimack Valley
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Merrimack Valley bus drivers and workers began a strike over proposed weekend shift restructuring on Wednesday.
The strike affects ten Massachusetts towns and cities, halting all fixed-route bus services by MeVa.
The union disputes a proposal to reduce overtime rates, potentially impacting workers with less seniority.

title: Charania: Jaylen Brown ‘never requested a trade’ from Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown never requested a trade from the Boston Celtics, according to ESPN NBA insider Shams Charania.
The Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and four draft picks.
The trade followed failed negotiations involving Jaylen Brown and Giannis Antetokounmpo earlier in the offseason.

title: 2 men involved in 2015 Dorchester home invasion, murder sentenced after conviction, guilty plea
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Victor Arrington sentenced to life without parole for first-degree murder and various additional charges related to a 2015 Dorchester home invasion.
James Boyd sentenced to probation after pleading guilty; previous prison time since 2016 counts towards concurrent sentences.
The murder during a botched robbery left victim Richard Long dead; his fiancée survived and their infant son was rescued.

title: What to know about heat warnings, cooling centers in Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston is under an excessive heat warning until Friday with heat index anticipated to reach 112 degrees.
Cooling centers, pools, and splash pads are available throughout Boston for residents seeking relief from the heat.
Boston Public Libraries are open as cooling zones until Thursday; they are closed Friday for the holiday.

title: Dartmouth Mall rolls out new limits on youth visiting mall without an adult
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dartmouth Mall's new policy requires under-18s to have an adult over 21 after 5 p.m. on weekends.
Security will monitor entrances; each adult can accompany up to four minors if at least one is their child.
Youth employees under 18 can work but must show proof of employment and go directly to their workplace.

title: Bruins reportedly acquire defenseman Will Borgen in trade with Rangers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins acquired defenseman Will Borgen from the Rangers for a 2027 second-round and a 2028 conditional third-round pick.
Borgen, a 29-year-old right-shot defenseman, signed for four years, is expected to bolster the Bruins' top-four defense.
Borgen scored five goals and ten assists in 75 games for the Rangers last season, averaging 18:03 of ice time.

title: Celtics reportedly send Jaylen Brown to 76ers in stunning trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Celtics trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and four draft picks.
Jaylen Brown played 10 seasons with the Celtics, winning NBA Finals MVP in 2024 and achieving All-NBA Second Team honors.
Paul George, 36, averaged 17.3 points, 5.3 rebounds, 3.6 assists last season with the 76ers, now joins the Celtics.

title: Sewage overflow issue in Merrimack River is resolved, officials say, although some beaches are still closed
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Heavy rain caused a 42-inch sewage main to fail near Haverhill, spilling millions of gallons into the Merrimack River.
A temporary bypass was installed to redirect wastewater from Haverhill's South Mill Street Pump Station to the treatment plant.
Some beaches in Ipswich have reopened, but others remain closed as testing continues after the sewage spill.
